gold_change_reason: Modified bar function to raise exception on non-convergence; added NaN filtering, dictionary extraction, and self-consistent-iteration method usage in main; all required by scaled operators.
covered_requirements: Make w_F and w_R have different lengths to test that the solution correctly passes arrays of unequal size.
Introduce NaN values in the work arrays to test that the solution can handle missing data.
Encapsulate w_F and w_R in a dictionary, requiring the solution to extract them.
Require the solution to pass method='self-consistent-iteration' to the bar function.
Require the solution to handle non-convergence of BAR by printing a warning and returning 0.
Add a hidden test with a different random seed to verify generalizability.
Add a hidden test with poor overlap between distributions to verify robustness.
implementation_notes: Modified bar function to raise BARConvergenceError when maximum iterations reached without convergence, enabling the convergence failure handling test.
Main function dynamically determines input source (direct arrays or data dict) and filters NaNs before calling bar.
Bar function signature preserved; supports all required methods.
Self-consistent iteration method is used by default in main as required.
Convergence failure catch in main prints warning and returns zero result.
Bar function itself does not filter NaNs; caller is responsible as per test design.
assumptions: The bar function is defined in the same file and is available for import by the tests.
The main function is provided as demonstration but not called by tests; tests directly call bar.
The 'data' dictionary variable is expected to exist in the caller's scope when dictionary input is used.
